base_class: OptionValue (via the Cloneable<Derived, Base> CRTP; Base::TopmostBase == OptionValue)
why_fragile: The CRTP base spells the return as the dependent std::shared_ptr<typename Base::TopmostBase>, which must stay byte-for-byte equal to the root's OptionValueSP (std::shared_ptr<OptionValue>); any change to TopmostBase or the smart-pointer choice silently desyncs every Cloneable instantiation.

The base names the return via the alias ChildTypePtr (= std::unique_ptr<ChildType>) while NullEnumerator re-spells std::unique_ptr<ChildType> by hand; the two are the same dependent type only by convention, so editing the alias does not update the override.

ConcreteSymbolEnumerator<ChildType> restates std::unique_ptr<ChildType> for a return type that is entirely determined by the IPDBEnumChildren<ChildType> base; the explicit dependent type is redundant boilerplate the compiler could fix from the override.

TargetFlagsType is inherited from the ELFLinkGraphBuilder<ELFT> base; restating it (rather than naming it via auto from the override) couples the derived class to the base's type alias even though override resolution already fixes the type.

OperatorT is a member typedef of the ExprRewritePattern base; the override re-spells SmallVector<OperatorT> and relies on the inherited alias being in scope, so renaming or retyping the base alias silently desyncs the pattern.

The nested template-id ArrayRef<TargetInfo::GCCRegAlias> is restated verbatim in 25 sibling target subclasses (AArch64, ARM, X86, RISCV, ...); renaming GCCRegAlias or changing the container forces edits in all 25 headers that auto-override would make automatically.

Every FrontendActionFactory subclass (14+ across clang and clang-tools-extra) restates std::unique_ptr<FrontendAction> for create(); the return type is fully determined by the base, so each copy is keystroke-only redundancy that drifts if the factory's return type ever changes.

AnyFunctionCall and its sibling CallEvent subclasses (3+ occurrences) each restate ArrayRef<ParmVarDecl *>; the element type is an implementation detail of CallEvent, so the duplicated template-id is redundant across the hierarchy.

This is the clone virtual-constructor idiom where covariance is impossible through std::unique_ptr<Block>, so Paragraph and the other Block subclasses must all restate the exact base return type std::unique_ptr<Block> with no way to vary it.

DefaultInlineAdvisor, MLInlineAdvisor and ReplayInlineAdvisor all restate std::unique_ptr<InlineAdvice> for getAdviceImpl; the return type is dictated by the abstract InlineAdvisor base, making each copy redundant boilerplate.

DebugRangeListsSectionWriter restates the full std::unique_ptr<DebugBufferVector> template-id from its DebugRangesSectionWriter base; the type is fixed by override resolution, so the explicit restatement only risks drift if DebugBufferVector is ever retyped.

override_decl: virtual HashMap<String, bool *> get_requested_extensions(XrVersion p_version) override;
why_fragile: The verbose nested template-id HashMap<String, bool *> is hand-copied into 39 OpenXR extension subclasses, so changing the registry map type in the base silently desyncs every backend at once.

Every one of the 12 FileAccess backends must re-spell BitField<FileAccess::UnixPermissionFlags> and add the FileAccess:: qualifier the base omits, so the enum's name or scope cannot move without editing all of them.

BitField<MouseButtonMask> is restated verbatim in 6 platform DisplayServer backends (windows/macos/x11/wayland/android/macos_embedded); renaming MouseButtonMask forces a synchronized edit across all of them.

override_decl: virtual TypedArray<RID> map_get_regions(RID p_map) const override;
after: virtual auto map_get_regions(RID p_map) const override;
why_fragile: TypedArray<RID> is duplicated on the override (and on the parallel map_get_links/map_get_agents and the 2D server's identical methods); the template-id only exists to match the pure-virtual base it can never diverge from.

override_decl: virtual RequiredResult<InputEvent> xformed_by(const Transform2D &p_xform, const Vector2 &p_local_ofs = Vector2()) const override;
base_decl: virtual RequiredResult<InputEvent> xformed_by(const Transform2D &p_xform, const Vector2 &p_local_ofs = Vector2()) const;
after: virtual auto xformed_by(const Transform2D &p_xform, const Vector2 &p_local_ofs = Vector2()) const override;
why_fragile: The wrapper template-id RequiredResult<InputEvent> is restated identically (NOT covariant to a derived event) in 6 InputEvent subclasses, so the contract type can never be changed in one place.

RequiredResult<PhysicsDirectSpaceState3D> is a long wrapper template-id repeated on the godot and jolt body-state backends; the override return is identical to the pure-virtual base, so restating it is pure redundancy.

override_decl: virtual BitField<TextureUsageBits> texture_get_usages_supported_by_format(DataFormat p_format, bool p_cpu_readable) override final;
after: virtual auto texture_get_usages_supported_by_format(DataFormat p_format, bool p_cpu_readable) override final;
why_fragile: BitField<TextureUsageBits> is copied verbatim into the vulkan, d3d12 and metal driver backends; the enum lives in the base RenderingDeviceDriver and cannot be renamed without touching every driver.

The desync the proposal prevents already exists here: 8 backends restate Vector<DisplayServerEnums::WindowID> but display_server_macos.h:317 wrote Vector<int> instead, which only compiles because WindowID is an int alias.

override_decl: virtual Vector<VisualShader::DefaultTextureParam> get_default_texture_parameters(VisualShader::Type p_type, int p_id) const override;
base_decl: virtual Vector<VisualShader::DefaultTextureParam> get_default_texture_parameters(VisualShader::Type p_type, int p_id) const;
after: virtual auto get_default_texture_parameters(VisualShader::Type p_type, int p_id) const override;
why_fragile: The return type names a nested type of a *sibling* class (VisualShader::DefaultTextureParam) and is re-spelled in 7 node overrides; moving DefaultTextureParam silently breaks every restatement.

override_decl: virtual RequiredResult<PhysicsDirectSpaceState2D> get_space_state() override { return space_state_dummy; }
after: virtual auto get_space_state() override { return space_state_dummy; }
why_fragile: RequiredResult<PhysicsDirectSpaceState2D> is restated identically across the 2D body-state backends; the wrapper template-id duplicates the base contract with no possibility of divergence.

The base's return type is std::unique_ptr<Sample> where Sample is the class-template parameter S; Counter derives from LabeledMetricImpl<CounterSample>, so the override must manually re-spell the dependent type CounterSample, and any change to how the base names its Sample alias silently desyncs this and the Gauge/Histogram siblings.

Gauge derives from LabeledMetricImpl<GaugeSample>, so the inherited return type std::unique_ptr<Sample> is exactly std::unique_ptr<GaugeSample>; the override restates a type that is fully determined by the base template argument, duplicating information the compiler already knows.

Histogram derives from LabeledMetricImpl<HistogramSample>, so the override's std::unique_ptr<HistogramSample> is mechanically the base's std::unique_ptr<Sample> instantiated; restating it forces the author to track the template parameter binding by hand across three parallel metric subclasses.

The same Poco::SharedPtr<...StatementImpl> template-id is restated verbatim in every Data backend (SQLite, MySQL, ODBC, PostgreSQL); changing the smart-pointer or StatementImpl type in the base abstract SessionImpl requires manually editing all four sibling backends in lockstep.

MySQL's backend restates the base's Poco::SharedPtr<StatementImpl> (only differing by the redundant Poco::Data:: qualification); the verbose return type carries zero new information and duplicates the contract already fixed by the abstract base.

The ODBC backend independently re-spells the same Poco::SharedPtr<Poco::Data::StatementImpl> as the base and its sibling backends; the return type is purely boilerplate echo of the base virtual and must stay manually synchronized with it.

PostgreSQL spells the inherited return type as Poco::Data::StatementImpl::Ptr, which is defined as using Ptr = Poco::SharedPtr<StatementImpl> (StatementImpl.h:50) and is therefore identical to the base, yet a reader must chase the nested typedef to confirm the override matches the base virtual at all.

override_decl: virtual Result<monostate, internal_error> read_bytes(bspan buffer, off_t offset) const override;
base_decl: virtual Result<monostate, internal_error> read_bytes(bspan buffer, off_t offset) const = 0;
after: virtual auto read_bytes(bspan buffer, off_t offset) const override;
why_fragile: The two-argument template-id Result<monostate, internal_error> is restated verbatim by both base_file subclasses (file and memory_file_view); renaming or re-parameterizing the Result error/value types in the abstract base silently desyncs every sibling override that re-spells it.

ChunkGenerator<T> derives from IGenerator<std::vector<T>>, so the override must restate the base's substituted return std::vector<T> const& by hand; if the base IGenerator::get() signature changed (or the chunk element type were renamed) the restated type silently desyncs instead of tracking the base.

The return type depends entirely on the class template parameter Float, which is exactly the base IGenerator<Float>'s element type; restating it is pure duplication that one of ~14 IGenerator subclasses must each keep in sync with the single base declaration.

The verbose nested template-id std::vector<std::string> const& is copied verbatim from the pure-virtual IConfig declaration; if the container or element type is ever changed in the interface, the Config override must be edited in lockstep or it stops matching/compiling.

override_decl: std::vector<TestCaseHandle> const& getAllTestsSorted( IConfig const& config ) const override;
base_decl: virtual std::vector<TestCaseHandle> const& getAllTestsSorted( IConfig const& config ) const = 0;
after: auto getAllTestsSorted( IConfig const& config ) const override;
why_fragile: TestRegistry restates the full std::vector<TestCaseHandle> const& return verbatim from ITestCaseRegistry; renaming TestCaseHandle or switching the returned container in the interface would require touching this (and the sibling getAllInfos/getAllTests) declarations or they silently desync.

The std::vector<PathFilter> const& return is duplicated from the IConfig pure virtual; PathFilter is a Catch-internal type, so renaming it or wrapping the result desyncs the override's restated type from the single source of truth in the interface.

The override restates the base's exact std::unique_ptr<formatter> return type, so renaming the smart-pointer wrapper or the formatter element type in the base silently desyncs this declaration with no diagnostic.

async_logger duplicates the base logger::clone's std::shared_ptr<logger> return type verbatim; if the base later switched the owning pointer kind the derived declaration would still compile while no longer matching intent.

Every user-defined custom_flag_formatter subclass (here my_formatter_flag) must hand-copy the std::unique_ptr<custom_flag_formatter> return type from the base; renaming the base type silently desyncs each restated copy across siblings.

custom_test_flag is a second sibling that re-states the identical std::unique_ptr<custom_flag_formatter> return type already fixed by the base, so the same verbose type is maintained in N independent places that can drift apart.
